description: A signal crossing clock domains without a synchronizer or timing constraint, risking intermittent metastability failures.
body: Failure mode: a signal generated in one clock feeds logic in another with no synchronizer and no false-path/max-delay constraint, so metastability causes rare, non-reproducible field failures. Detection signals: CDC tool flags an unconstrained crossing; intermittent hardware glitches not seen in sim; a multi-bit bus synchronized bit-by-bit. Do not connect cross-domain signals directly or rely on a single synchronizer for buses. Safer fallback: add a 2+ flop synchronizer for control or an async FIFO/gray code for buses, add timing constraints, and confirm via a CDC report.
